title: Creation of HBSS Wash Buffer
author: Brusko Laboratory
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8xbhxin

steps.instruction: Open a sterile 1 liter (L) Rapid-Flow Sterile Disposable Filter Unit with a 0.2 micrometer (µm) PES Membrane. Rapid-Flow Sterile Disposable Filter Unit with PES Membrane (Filtration System, Nalgene, 5670020, https://www.fishersci.com/shop/products/thermo-scientific-nalgene-rapid-flow-sterile-disposable-filter-units-1000ml-pore-size-0-20-m-membrane-90mm/0974103 , https://s3.amazonaws.com/protocols-files/public/17202527ff76dc2c9ec9e1eeebb482b119610ca4edff8c34edd016bb40a1eab4/btir93j6.jpg )
Remove the lid of the filter flask and add 940 mL of (1x) HBSS to the filter. HBSS (1x) Gibco - Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #14175-095

Add a single 50 mL aliquot of FBS and a 10 mL aliquot of Penicillin/Streptomycin (Pen/Strep) to the HBSS in the filter system Fetal Bovine Serum (FBS) Genesee Scientific Catalog #25-550 Penicillin/Streptomycin Corning Catalog #30-002-CL
Add 10 µL of 0.5 Molarity (M) EDTA to the solution. EDTA (0.5 Molar) Boston Bioproducts Catalog #BM-150
Process the final solution through the 0.2 µm PES membrane using a positive-pressure system.

Once the solution has been filtered, remove the filter from the flask by unscrewing the filter and dispose of the filter into biohazardous waste. Since this procedure makes use of penicillin and streptomycin, any disposable products that come into contact with these antibiotics should be disposed of in biohazards waste to prevent the creation of antibiotic-resistant bacteria.

Remove the flask lid from the secondary container and securely screw the cap onto the flask. Label the flask containing the sterile HBSS Wash Buffer with the following information: "HBSS with FBS, EDTA, Pen/Strep" as well as your name and the date.
Store the HBSS Wash Buffer in 4 °C .
